## Sentinel is now your polkit authentication agent

v0.4 makes `sentinel-polkit-agent` the registered polkit auth agent
for the user session. `pam_sentinel.so` consults the agent over a
Unix-domain socket before deciding to spawn a dialog, so
polkit-mediated actions (`pkexec`, `cosmic-settings` privileged
panels, mounting disks, …) render exactly **one** Sentinel UI
instead of racing with cosmic-osd / polkit-gnome / polkit-kde.

The 0.3.x bypass token was retired. Trust between the agent and the
PAM module is now established via `SO_PEERCRED` on
`$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/sentinel-agent.sock` — peer must be uid 0 with
`comm` matching `polkit-agent-helper-1`. Approvals are one-shot, with
a 5-second TTL.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> v0.3.0 and v0.3.1 are yanked. Upgrade directly from 0.2.x to 0.4.0.
> A second root shell (`pkexec bash` or different TTY) is recommended
> during install — see the wiki **Troubleshooting** page.

## Dialog rework

The confirmation dialog has been redesigned around a single card
listing the requesting executable. Click **▸ Show details** to expand:

- **Command** — full command with arguments (`/proc/<pid>/cmdline`)
- **PID**, **Working directory**
- **Requested by** — invoking user
- **Action** — polkit `action_id` or PAM service name

The card is bounded at 520×640 with internal scrolling; per-field text
is hard-truncated at 4096 chars so a megabyte-long argv can't wreck
layout. Sudo and pkexec render the same surface — same wording,
same fields, same sizes.

## Other notable changes

- **Calling-process resolution** — PAM now reports the privileged
  binary itself (`getpid()`, e.g. `/usr/bin/sudo`) instead of its
  parent shell, so the displayed command line is what's actually
  about to run.
- **Transactional install simplified** — fresh installs only; no
  0.2/0.3 migration code, no systemd user unit (XDG autostart drives
  the agent so it inherits the graphical sessionid; systemd user
  units fail polkit's session check).
- **Shell completions and man pages** for `sentinel-helper`,
  `sentinel-polkit-agent`, `sentinel.conf(5)`, and `pam_sentinel(8)`
  now ship with every package.
- Distribution updates: AUR `sentinel` + `sentinel-git`, Debian
  `.deb`, RPM `.rpm`, generic tarball, NixOS flake — all on 0.4.0.

## Cosmic users

If you previously `chmod -x`'d `/usr/bin/cosmic-osd` to suppress its
polkit prompt, you can leave it as-is. The Sentinel agent's XDG
autostart entry registers first; cosmic-osd's same-subject
registration attempt will fail and it'll back off.

If you'd like to restore cosmic-osd's executable bit:

```bash
sudo chmod +x /usr/bin/cosmic-osd
```

Either way works post-0.4.

## Verify

```bash
pgrep -af sentinel-polkit-agent
ls -la $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/sentinel-agent.sock
pkexec true                                # exactly one Sentinel dialog
sudo -k && sudo true                       # also gated, same UI
journalctl -t sentinel-polkit-agent -f     # live agent log
journalctl -t pam_sentinel -f              # live PAM log
```

Full installation, configuration, architecture, and troubleshooting
docs at <https://github.com/atayozcan/sentinel/wiki>.
